commit.message:
fix: stream volume control + proper AudioWorklet noise gate

Stream volume fixes:
- Video element always muted=true; audio exclusively through remoteScreenAudios
  element (was double-playing: video AND audio element both at full volume)
- Direct mute/volume slider overlay on screen-share video (bottom bar on hover)
- muteRemoteScreenStream() added to webrtc.ts for per-stream muting
- Saved stream volume preference restored on stream attach
- Vol menu stream slider now also has dedicated mute/unmute button

Noise gate (AudioWorklet):
- public/noise-processor.js: new AudioWorkletProcessor
  • Adaptive noise floor tracker (2 s minimum window, fast drop / slow rise)
  • Gate threshold = max(MIN_THRESHOLD, noiseFloor × 3.5) — adapts to room
  • Soft-knee transition (quadratic, 25%–100% of threshold) to avoid clicks
  • 15 ms gain smoothing to prevent artefacts
  • HPF 80 Hz + LPF 8 kHz filters in chain (cut rumble & hiss)
- acquireMic() now pipes raw stream through the worklet before sending to peers
- Speaking indicator runs on raw stream (accurate even when gate is closing)
- noisePipelineRef tracks cleanup across re-acquires and call hangup
- Graceful fallback: if AudioWorklet unavailable (HTTP / old browser), uses
  raw stream with browser built-in noiseSuppression instead

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: multi-agent architecture — 3 specialized agents, long-term memory, coaching state machine

Phase 2 implementation:
- Coaching state machine (7 training phases: healthy_loading, recovery_week,
  adaptation_needed, injury_risk, taper, race_week, post_race) with data-driven
  transitions based on alerts, recovery score, and race countdown
- Long-term athlete memory with SQLite + ChromaDB dual storage — episodic,
  semantic, and procedural memories extracted via Claude Haiku after each conversation
- 3 specialized agents (DailyCoach, Analysis, Planning) with filtered tool subsets
  and domain-specific system prompts, routed via zero-cost keyword intent classification
- Agent orchestrator as drop-in replacement for CoachingEngine (same respond() signature)
- Reusable run_tool_loop() extracted from engine for shared use by all agents
- create_workout tool for intervals.icu workout creation (planning agent)
- State machine integrated into morning reactor for phase-aware coaching

commit.message:
Crucible: detect parent-child from blocks/blocked_by instead of requiring parent field (Forge-q3d) (#125)

* feat: detect epic parent-child from blocks dependency graph

Crucible epic branch detection now works via two paths:
1. Parent field (legacy): child.Parent points to epic bead
2. Blocks field (preferred): child blocks an epic-type bead

The blocks-based detection solves the chicken-and-egg problem where
beads with parent set are hidden from `bd ready`, making the poller
unable to discover them. Children that block an epic are now
automatically routed through the epic's feature branch.

Also reorders ResolveBlocks before ResolveEpicBranches in the poll
loop so block data is available for epic detection, and updates the
manual dispatch path to use both detection methods.

Bead: Forge-q3d

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-q3d

- Use unwrapJSONArray in lookupEpicBranch before json.Unmarshal and log
  unmarshal errors (Comment 1)
- Call ResolveBlocks before ResolveEpicBranches in IPC run_bead path when
  Crucible is enabled, so beads with empty Blocks still get epic
  relationships resolved (Comment 2)
- Make lookupEpicBranch injectable via epicBranchLookupFunc and add unit
  tests for blocks-based epic detection including caching (Comment 3)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: implement comprehensive error handling & retry logic (Step 6)

Add robust retry system with exponential backoff, jitter, and dead-letter queue.

**Niveau 1 (Quick Wins):**
- Add jitter to exponential backoff (prevent thundering herd)
- Read Retry-After headers from HTTP responses
- Categorize errors by type (RATE_LIMITED, TIMEOUT, CONNECTION_ERROR, etc.)
- Better error responses to frontend with error_type + recoverable flags

**Niveau 2 (Robustness):**
- New cards/retry_utils.py: Retry utilities with tenacity
  - categorize_error(): Map exceptions to error types
  - calculate_backoff_with_jitter(): Exponential backoff + jitter
  - extract_retry_after(): Read Retry-After header
  - retry_with_backoff(): Execute with automatic retry
  - @retryable_task decorator: For Celery tasks

- Update import_card_task, import_set_task with Celery autoretry
  - autoretry_for=(requests.RequestException,)
  - retry_kwargs={'max_retries': 3, 'countdown': 5}
  - Better error categorization in responses

- Improve _scrape_store() with better retry logic
  - Use retry_with_backoff() for clean code
  - Categorize errors: RATE_LIMITED, SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE, etc.
  - Integrate with circuit breaker on persistent failures

- Add TaskFailureLog model (dead-letter queue)
  - Track failed tasks with error details
  - Store task args/kwargs for manual retry
  - Admin interface for viewing and marking resolved
  - Management command: python manage.py retry_failed_tasks

- Celery signal handler: Automatically log task failures
  - Maps to task_failure signal
  - Categorizes error automatically

- Update Django admin
  - StoreCircuitBreakerAdmin: View circuit breaker states
  - TaskFailureLogAdmin: View, filter, bulk retry failed tasks

- Add retry_failed_tasks management command
  - Retry unresolved tasks by error type or task ID
  - Dry-run mode to preview retries

- Add tenacity==9.0.0 to requirements.txt
- Add celery task failure handling config in settings.py
- Add migration for TaskFailureLog model

**Documentation:**
- RETRY_AND_ERROR_HANDLING.md: Comprehensive guide with examples
- cards/tests/test_retry_utils.py: Unit tests for retry logic

revert(tree): remove cached_cursor_path and cached_filtered_paths

The cursor path cache and filtered paths cache added complexity without
sufficient benefit — call ordering issues (current_node_info running
before move_cursor) caused regressions. Revert to the simpler approach
while keeping other optimizations (cached_visible_count, tracing spans,
visible_nodes_in_range replacements, expand_dir_at helper).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: session persistence — auto-save/restore via localStorage

Quand l'URL ne change pas entre les étapes, la session est maintenant
sauvegardée automatiquement à chaque changement d'étape (switchStep).
Rechargement de la page → retour exact là où on était (emails générés,
brief, cible, angles, CTAs...).

- saveSession() — sérialise ST dans localStorage (kbFiles et KB permanent
  exclus, re-injectés à la restauration)
- clearSession() — efface la session (appelé sur retour au sélecteur)
- tryRestoreSession() — restaure l'état complet au DOMContentLoaded :
  UI de marque, niveau, type de campagne, CTAs, textarea brief, KB,
  emails (renderEmails), angles (renderAngles), step courant
- saveSession() déclenché aussi après modification d'email (applyModification)
- Toast "✅ Session restaurée" pour confirmer visuellement

commit.message:
fix: 3 critical pipeline bugs — query timeout, SOL price leak, ON CONFLICT

1. fill_outcomes statement timeout: Split single OR query (7 horizons)
   into per-horizon queries. The complex OR filter caused full-table scans
   on 20K+ pending snapshots, hitting Supabase statement_timeout every run.
   No labels were written since March 1st.

2. KCO Phase B SOL price leak: DexPaprika returns SOL price (~$85) for
   inverted pools. Added absolute median check (>$50 = reject) BEFORE
   the Birdeye fallback, so Birdeye (which uses token MINT directly)
   handles these tokens correctly.

3. price_refresh ON CONFLICT mismatch: Unique constraint is
   (symbol, time_window, token_address) but upsert used only
   (symbol, time_window). Added token_address to both the row data
   and the on_conflict clause. Price refresh failed every 3 minutes.

commit.message:
fix: add cache-busting to post-deploy smoke tests to prevent stale 404s (#5339)

Post-deploy smoke tests for /tim profile were failing with 404 due to
Vercel CDN edge cache serving stale responses after deploys. Added
cache-busting query parameters (_cb=timestamp) and strengthened cache
headers (no-store, must-revalidate, Pragma: no-cache) to all curl
requests in ci-public-profile-smoke and canary-health-gate workflows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix flashcard swipe stale closure bug

Use a ref (dragXRef) alongside state to track drag distance so that
onTouchEnd/onMouseUp always read the latest value. Previously, React's
batched rendering could cause the end handlers to see a stale dragX
from state, causing swipes near the threshold to fail silently.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix five functional issues from code review

1. Broaden Jira key pattern to accept digits and underscores in
   project keys (e.g. APP2-123, TEAM_A-7). Pattern is now
   ^[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*-[0-9]+$ matching real Jira key formats.

2. codex-review-plan.sh: Capture Codex stderr and exit code
   separately. Exit 2 signals a Codex execution failure (auth,
   network, crash) so Phase 3 surfaces the error instead of
   burning 5 plan-revision loops on empty output.

3. codex-review-code.sh: Exclude .claude/swe-flow-*.md state files
   from both the git diff and untracked file scan, so the 30k
   truncation budget is spent on actual implementation code.

4. codex-review-code.sh: Same exit-code fix as plan review — exit 2
   for Codex failures, exit 1 only for real review findings.

5. record-video.sh: After backgrounding screencapture, wait 2s and
   verify the process is still alive. Reports a clear error with
   permission instructions if it exited immediately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
